title: Centaurea microlopha
intro: Centaurea microlopha, also known as Mountain Centaury or Small-headed Knapweed, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as digestive issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea microlopha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Centaurea microlopha is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to soothe digestive issues and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Centaurea microlopha is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Cardueae and is part of the genus Centaurea. The plant is further categorized under the subgenus Centaurea. Centaurea microlopha is native to Greece, where it can be found growing in mountainous regions. This classification reflects its unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.
